name: serde
what_it_does: The standard Rust serialization/deserialization framework. A single `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` attribute makes any Rust struct serializable to/from JSON, YAML, TOML, MessagePack, CBOR, and 20+ other formats. Zero-cost abstractions — Serde generates format-specific code at compile time with no runtime reflection overhead.
use_cases: Serialize and deserialize LLM API request/response types to/from JSON in Rust agent code with derive macros
Define strongly-typed configuration structs that auto-deserialize from TOML or YAML config files
Convert between data formats (JSON ↔ MessagePack) in agent data pipeline stages without runtime overhead
Validate incoming webhook payloads against typed Rust structs — deserialization failures become type errors
Implement custom serialization logic for domain types (UUID, DateTime, enum discriminants) via serde attributes
not_for: Dynamic schema handling where field names are unknown at compile time — use serde_json::Value for untyped JSON
High-level schema validation beyond types — use validator crate for business rule validation after deserialization
Non-Rust codebases — Serde is Rust-specific; language-agnostic serialization uses Protocol Buffers or Avro
best_when: You need efficient, ergonomic serialization in Rust — Serde is the universal standard and required by virtually all Rust JSON/YAML/TOML libraries.
avoid_when: You need runtime dynamic schemas or reflection-based serialization — Serde is compile-time only; use serde_json::Value for fully dynamic JSON.

auth.notes: Local serialization library — no authentication required.

pricing.notes: MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license. One of the most downloaded Rust crates. Foundation of the Rust serialization ecosystem.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — zero-overhead compile-time code generation. Comparable to hand-written serialization code.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Deserialization errors include field names, expected types, and source positions (for JSON with serde_json). Compile-time derive errors are sometimes verbose but precise. Custom error messages via #[serde(rename)] and custom visitors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure serialization library — deterministic, no side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: serde derive requires the serde crate with features = ["derive"] in Cargo.toml — the derive feature is not enabled by default; a common beginner mistake
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] renames all fields but doesn't handle irregular names — use #[serde(rename = "fieldName")] for exceptions
Option<T> fields are serialized as null by default — use #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] to omit absent fields in JSON APIs
Enum serialization format is unexpected by default — adjacently tagged, internally tagged, and untagged representations require explicit #[serde(tag)] attributes to match API expectations
serde does not validate field values beyond type coercion — a String field accepts any string; add the validator crate for business rule validation
Deserializing unknown fields causes errors by default for structs — add #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] to reject extra fields or omit it to silently ignore them

security_score_components.security_notes: Rust memory safety eliminates buffer overflow risks in deserialization. Deeply nested JSON can cause stack overflow — set recursion limits for untrusted input. Use #[serde(skip)] to exclude sensitive fields from serialization.
